entries.title: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
entries.snippet: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

entries.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
entries.snippet: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

entries.title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
entries.snippet: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

entries.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
entries.snippet: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

entries.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
entries.snippet: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

entries.title: Mass trial for 486 alleged gang members begins in El Salvador
entries.snippet: Footage released by the attorney general's office shows large groups of men in prison attending the trial via video link.

entries.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
entries.snippet: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

entries.title: Role of US officials killed in crash in Mexico under scrutiny
entries.snippet: Two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

entries.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids
entries.snippet: Several people were also injured in the incident on Monday at the popular tourist site, say Mexican officials, weeks before the country hosts the Fifa World Cup.

entries.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

entries.title: Arrests in Nigeria after abduction of local monarch 'held for ransom'
entries.snippet: The kidnappers raided his palace on Saturday and are believed to be holding him in a forest.

entries.title: The Onion launches new effort to turn Infowars into parody website
entries.snippet: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

entries.title: South Africa's police boss charged in connection with controversial health contract
entries.snippet: Fannie Masemola is accused of failing in his duties to provide proper oversight in his role.

entries.title: Ofcom investigating Telegram over child sexual abuse material concerns
entries.snippet: The popular messaging service told the BBC in a statement it "categorically denies Ofcom's accusations".

entries.title: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
entries.snippet: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

entries.title: Blue Origin rocket grounded after satellite 'mishap'
entries.snippet: The firm founded by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is investigating the failed launch.

entries.title: Police gunfight with favela gang traps 200 tourists on hilltop
entries.snippet: The access route to Morro Dois Irmãos was blocked during the operation in Vidigal, leaving scores of alarmed sightseers stuck on the hill.

entries.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
entries.snippet: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

entries.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?
entries.snippet: How Ternus responds to challenges facing Apple will go a long way to deciding the tech giant's future, writes Zoe Kleinman.

entries.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
entries.snippet: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

entries.title: Virginia voters OK a Democratic effort to redraw the state's congressional map
entries.snippet: Virginia voters have delivered a significant win to Democrats, as the party aims to pick up four more U.S. House seats in the state.

entries.title: Southern Poverty Law Center indicted on federal fraud charges
entries.snippet: The Justice Department alleges that the SPLC improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups.

entries.title: Instead of civil war, a naked mole rat colony changed queens peacefully
entries.snippet: These matriarchal rodents often have bloody succession wars to replace their queen. But in a colony in California, Queen Tere ceded the throne to her daughter, Arwen, without violence.

entries.title: Florida AG launches criminal investigation into ChatGPT over FSU shooting
entries.snippet: Florida's attorney general is launching a criminal investigation into the alleged role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting at Florida State University last year.

entries.title: Florida Democrat Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick, facing possible expulsion, resigns
entries.snippet: The Florida Democrat faced a potential expulsion vote in the House as she prepares for a federal trial on allegations that she stole disaster funds and used some of the money to finance her campaign.

entries.title: Want to lighten your mental load? First, let go of these gender myths
entries.snippet: "Men can't see the mess." "Women are better at chores." These myths position women to take on more emotional thinking, says researcher Leah Ruppanner. She shares what works to reclaim your headspace.

entries.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
entries.snippet: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

entries.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
entries.snippet: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

entries.title: Ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon
entries.snippet: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

entries.title: The hidden power keeping wages low
entries.snippet: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to understanding today's inequality.

entries.title: Appeals court rules Texas can require public schools to display Ten Commandments in class
entries.snippet: Texas can require the Ten Commandments to be displayed in public school classrooms, a U.S. appeals court ruled Tuesday in a victory for conservatives who have long sought to incorporate more religion into schools.

entries.title: WATCH: Trump addresses NCAA Collegiate National Champions at White House
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump on Tuesday repeated his call for Congress to pass legislation that would rein in college sports at a time athletes are allowed to move freely from school to school and command salaries that put athletic departments in financial peril.

entries.title: WATCH: Justice Department charges SPLC with fraud over paid informant program
entries.snippet: The Southern Poverty Law Center was indicted Tuesday on federal fraud charges alleging it improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups, acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said.

entries.title: South Korean police seek to arrest K-pop mogul behind BTS over alleged fraud
entries.snippet: South Korean police said Tuesday they are seeking to arrest music mogul Bang Si-Hyuk, chairman of the agency behind K-pop supergroup BTS, as they expand an investigation into allegations that he illegally gained more than $100 million in an investor fraud scheme.

entries.title: Man convicted of setting neighbor on fire during 1990 burglary is executed in Florida
entries.snippet: Florida's fifth execution of 2026 followed a record 19 executions in the state last year. Republican Gov. Ron DeSantis oversaw more executions in a single year in 2025 than any other Florida governor since the death penalty was reinstated in 1976.

entries.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire as talks to end war stall
entries.snippet: President Trump said late Tuesday he would extend the ceasefire with Iran as negotiations over the next round of talks seem to be at an impasse. Trump said Pakistani mediators asked him to extend the truce as the sides remain far apart on terms for ending the war. White House cor

entries.title: News Wrap: DOJ announces criminal charges against Southern Poverty Law Center
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Tuesday, the Justice Department announced criminal charges against the Southern Poverty Law Center over its use of paid informants to infiltrate extremist groups, the Pentagon revealed details about its $1.5 trillion budget request and Defense Secretary Pete Hegs

entries.title: Gunman at Mexican pyramids carried materials related to 1999 Columbine massacre
entries.snippet: The gunman who opened fire on tourists at Mexico's iconic Teotihuacan pyramids carried materials that were apparently related to the 1999 shooting at Columbine High School, authorities said Tuesday, a day after the attack that killed a Canadian woman and left at least 13 people i

entries.title: Fed nominee Warsh questioned on independence from Trump and personal wealth
entries.snippet: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve, Kevin Warsh, was on Capitol Hill facing a slew of lawmaker questions on his monetary policy and independence from Trump. But the president's pressure campaign against Jerome Powell threatens to freeze Warsh's nomination. Amna Na

entries.title: Rep. Cherfilus-McCormick resigns ahead of ethics probe into use of campaign funds
entries.snippet: Another scandal-plagued member of Congress resigned on Tuesday, exactly one week after a pair of her peers did the same. Democrat Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick of Florida faced an ethics investigation into her use of campaign funds. Lisa Desjardins reports.

entries.title: In rare interview, top Hezbollah leader rejects disarmament, calls for Israeli withdrawal
entries.snippet: Representatives from the governments of Lebanon and Israel will meet again this week in Washington for peace talks. But Hezbollah is not involved. The full disarmament of the Iran-backed group is a central part of the discussions, but in a rare interview, top Hezbollah official W

entries.title: Apple prepares for new era after Tim Cook
entries.snippet: Apple is entering a major transition after CEO Tim Cook announced he's stepping down following 15 years at the helm of one of the world's most influential tech companies. Cook will hand leadership to John Ternus, the company's head of hardware engineering. It comes as Apple confr

entries.title: Gen Z men and women on why they're sharply divided over reproductive rights
entries.snippet: A majority of Americans think abortion should be legal in all or most cases, but there is a gap between men and women on the issue. That divide is perhaps clearest among Generation Z, those born between 1997 and 2012. This split is part of a bigger picture about how Gen Z thinks

entries.title: New growth in logged forests inspires photographer's hope
entries.snippet: Scott Rickenberger is a photographer from North Bend, Washington, who shares his passion for the natural world and highlights how trees that were logged more than a century ago can be surprising sources of inspiration and hope. Julinna Wu of our journalism training program, PBS N

entries.title: U.S. officials killed in Mexico after anti-drug operation were working for CIA, AP sources say
entries.snippet: The lack of clarity from authorities reignited a debate over the extent of U.S. involvement in Mexico's security operations as Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum faces extreme pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump's administration to crack down on cartels.

entries.title: Approval of Trump on economy falls in new AP-NORC poll, as Iran war drives up prices
entries.snippet: The findings from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research show a president who is struggling with unfulfilled promises to tame inflation and testing Americans' patience with a conflict in the Middle East that has dragged on longer than expected.

entries.title: LISTEN: Supreme Court seems skeptical of limiting FCC's power to fine companies
entries.snippet: Telecommunications giants Verizon and AT&T appealed to the Supreme Court after the Federal Communications Commission found they sold customers' location data without proper safeguards and slapped the companies with hefty penalties totaling over $100 million.

entries.title: A timeline of Trump's shifting statements about how long the Iran war will last
entries.snippet: Since the U.S. and Israel launched the strikes against Iran Feb. 28, President Donald Trump has made contradictory statements about the timeline to end the war. He has repeatedly declared victory and then later threatened Iran.

entries.title: Japan lifts ban on lethal weapons exports in major change of its postwar pacifist policy
entries.snippet: The move comes as the country accelerates its military buildup in the face of growing security challenges in the region.

entries.title: Pope Leo criticizes colonization of minerals, 'lust for power' in Equatorial Guinea
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV has denounced the "colonization" of minerals and the "lust for power" in Equatorial Guinea.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Iran war: Trump says US extending ceasefire to give Iran more time to negotiate
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump said he was ​extending the Iran ​ceasefire until an Iranian ⁠proposal ​is ​submitted, ​and discussions ‌are concluded. A US ceasefire with Iran was due to expire on Wednesday evening. DW has more.

entries.title: Nigeria charges six over alleged Tinubu overthrow plot
entries.snippet: The alleged plot comes after 16 military officers stood trial in January over a coup attempt against president Bola Tinubu.

entries.title: Lebanon war puts pregnant women at risk
entries.snippet: Lebanon’s healthcare system is under severe strain despite a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Thousands of displaced pregnant women are struggling to access medical services.

entries.title: Zelenskyy says Druzhba oil pipeline to Europe repaired
entries.snippet: A multibillion euro aid package from the EU had been blocked over access to the pipeline, which brings Russian oil to countries like Hungary and Slovakia.

entries.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
entries.snippet: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

entries.title: UK moves to ban smoking for everyone born after 2008
entries.snippet: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by banning smoking for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

entries.title: What are Japan, South Korea learning from Hormuz disruption?
entries.snippet: The Strait of Hormuz blockade has shaken Tokyo and Seoul, highlighting their dependence on maritime trade for essential goods including food and fuel.

entries.title: Mars: NASA's Curiosity rover finds new organic matter in Gale Crater
entries.snippet: NASA's Curiosity rover has identified more organic compounds as scientists investigate any potential ancient life signs on Mars. It was exploring the massive Gale Crater also believed to be a now-dry lake.

entries.title: Nigeria's $2B power bailout tests Tinubu reforms
entries.snippet: Nigeria has approved billions to clear power sector debts. Analysts say the move may stabilize electricity supply, but deeper structural problems remain unresolved.

entries.title: India news: Delhi braces for first heatwave of the year
entries.snippet: The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert with temperatures over 40 degrees predicted for the coming days. Meanwhile, reports suggest India and the US are on the verge of a trade deal. DW has more.

entries.title: Taiwan blames pressure from China for nixed Africa trip
entries.snippet: The Taiwanese government says Beijing used economic coercion to convince three African nations to revoke permission for President Lai Ching-te to traverse their airspace.

entries.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
entries.snippet: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

entries.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the iPhone maker
entries.snippet: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

entries.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
entries.snippet: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

entries.title: Why cloud computing still runs on coal and gas
entries.snippet: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

entries.title: Japan loosens long-standing curbs on arms exports
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, a major shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

entries.title: Germany news: Trial begins in Istanbul over tourist deaths
entries.snippet: Six people have been charged with negligent homicide over the deaths of four members of a German family at a hotel in Turkey. Meanwhile, a major climate conference kicked off in Berlin. DW has more.

entries.title: South Korea: Police seek to arrest BTS agency chief over fraud allegations
entries.snippet: Police say HYBE chief Bang Si-hyuk secretly made millions from post-IPO share sales. The case comes as K-pop supergroup BTS embarks on a global tour.

entries.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
entries.snippet: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.
